acquisition method: We obtained the MS data by data dependent acquisition.
alkylation reagent: We then alkylated the reduced proteins with 18 mM iodoacetamide.
alkylation concentration: We then alkylated the reduced proteins with 18 mM iodoacetamide.
cleavage agent: We then digested the protein by adding trypsin (Sigma-Aldrich, St. Louis, MO, USA).
fragmentation method: we selected our top 12 most intense precursor ions in positive mode for ms^2 HCD fragmentation.
instrument: We measured the mass of the eluted peptides using a Q Exactive Orbitrap MS.
ionization type: The MS was coupled to the chromatography system via a nano-electrospray ion source.
labeling: We subsequently labelled the resulting peptides using the Tandem Mass Tag™ (TMT™) 10-plex mass tag kit.
reduction reagent: We first reduced 30 μg of protein from each of the three-independent biological replicates in each isolate with 10 mM tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphine (TCEP, Sigma-Aldrich, USA).
reduction concentration: We first reduced 30 μg of protein from each of the three-independent biological replicates in each isolate with 10 mM tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphine (TCEP, Sigma-Aldrich, USA).
